message:
fix: prevent MCP server self-kill on startup (#1381)

* fix: intelligence layer loads flat array from auto-memory store

The auto-memory-hook.mjs writes a flat JSON array to auto-memory-store.json,
but intelligence.cjs loadEntries() only checked for store.entries (object
wrapper format). This caused the vector store to load only 1 fallback
pattern from MEMORY.md instead of the full 1,300+ entries.

Now supports both formats: flat array and { entries: [...] } wrapper.
Also fixes deprecated Dirent.path usage (use parentPath first).

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

* fix: apply same loadEntries fix to helpers-generator.ts

The published CLI package generates intelligence.cjs during `init` via
helpers-generator.ts — it had the same flat-array bug. Users running
`npx claude-flow init` would get the broken version.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

* fix: statusline uses real data instead of hardcoded/estimated values

- Hooks: count actual hook entries instead of hardcoding total=17
- Vectors: count real entries from auto-memory-store.json, not file-size/2
- Tests: scan v3/ directory (depth 6), was missing ~2000 test files
- Swarm: only show active if state updated within 5 min (stale check)
- Security: flag as STALE if audit older than 7 days
- ADRs: count actual files (76), don't use stale compliance JSON (7)
- Intelligence: scale to 2000 entries = 100% instead of 100 entries

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

* fix: apply statusline accuracy fixes to generator (end-user path)

The statusline-generator.ts always overwrites the bundled statusline.cjs
during init, so all 6 accuracy fixes must be in both files:

- Hooks: count actual entries, not hardcode 17
- Vectors: read real entry count from store JSON
- Tests: scan depth 6, include v3/ directory
- Swarm: 5-min staleness check on metrics
- Security: PENDING when no audit run, STALE after 7 days
- ADRs: count files directly, skip stale compliance JSON

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

* fix: prevent MCP server self-kill on startup (#1380)

Three compounding bugs caused `mcp start` to crash with "Killed":

1. Self-kill: getStatus() reported current process as "running" for stdio
   transport, then start command sent SIGKILL to its own PID
2. Config crash: Zod schema rejected partial configs with non-object values
3. Hook exit: process.exitCode=0 was overridden by async errors

Fixes:
- Add isSelfDetected guard to skip kill when PID matches process.pid
- Add .passthrough() to SystemConfigSchema, merge partial configs with defaults
- Use process.exit(0) in .finally() for reliable hook exits
- Remove invalid hook events (TeammateIdle, TaskCompleted, PostCompact)
- Wrap hook commands in sh -c for cross-platform CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR expansion

Published as v3.5.39. Tested in Docker — clean startup, no warnings.

Closes #1380

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>
